entry.title: Breaking Out of Comfort Zones: The Learning Journey of Danielle Larocca
entry.content:
There is no doubt that the way we learn today has changed – dramatically. “I remember what it was like to attend SAP training classes back in the late 90’s”, Danielle Larocca recounts, “back then they were hosted onsite only and you would get paper-based certificates via a small ceremony each time.”  

It was 1995, and for Danielle, her SAP HCM and Payroll reporting experience was just about to start. She was working for Doubleday Direct, which later became a Bertelsmann AG and AOL Time Warner Partnership in Garden City, New York. The company had decided to implement SAP amidst fears that their legacy payroll system would cease functioning in the Y2K era. And as part of the implementation project, she was sent off for ABAP/4 training.  

Learning all about AI

Today, the learning experience looks very different for the global VP for Strategic Alliances. “Over the past several months, I have been dedicating my educational hours to all things AI.” What else would it be in the year 2024? The SAP Mentor started writing a series of blogs, ranging from AI-generated headshots, to functional video avatars and video language conversions. Her work – and learning experience – has become way more digital, fast-paced, and versatile compared to previous decades. “I have even let AI write a blog post for me and make a ppt for me", she highlights. “I also make an effort to share a weekly #AI Byte on LinkedIn that shares with others some key topics on AI that should be on their radar.” No doubt that the way she learns and the topics she learns about have changed.  

But there is more to is, says Danielle: “My life has changed in the sense that I have gone outside my comfort zone.” Moving away from HCM, Payroll, and reporting – her familiar turf – Danielle started to learn about technologies outside of her world, “to expand my horizon on the art of the possible with what technology can do.” Her latest achievement: The “Introducing SAP Business AI Commercial Model” learning journey on SAP Learning site, which rewarded her with an official record of achievement to demonstrate her expertise. 

Adapting to changing technologies is important

However, Danielle admits that learning a new skill sometimes comes with a small damper: “It is hard to A) carve out the time and B) learn something new but it is rewarding and exciting to be in the loop on the latest technological advancements that will transform our everyday lives.” As we get older, she states, “it's easy to settle into our homeostasis of what we know and what we are experts on and it's easy to stay in that comfort zone, but it does not allow for personal growth.” According to Danielle, we all must adapt and learn new things to survive and thrive, especially in an ever-changing technological evolution climate.   

“With all that said I have carved out time from my very busy schedule to learn something new each week.  I call it my education hours and they usually fall on a Sunday night.  I am committed to learning something new for 2 hours every week that is outside the scope of my day-to-day life and it involves some form of cutting-edge tech, for me its AI.” 

How do you learn? Share!

How do you tackle learning a new skill? Let us know about your strategy in the comments and start your next chapter of boosting your career with future-focused learning on the SAP Learning site today.

entry.title: 2H 2024 Release Highlights : Unlocking the Power of AI in SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content:
AI in Action

Artificial Intelligence is no longer just a buzzword—it’s reshaping how we work, especially in HR. SAP SuccessFactors continues to revolutionize HR technology with its AI-powered features in the 2H 2024 release. Artificial intelligence is seamlessly integrated to enhance productivity, personalization, and decision-making. Here’s a comprehensive look at the AI-driven functionalities that are making waves across multiple modules.

1. AI-Assisted Microsoft Teams Chat Integration

  • Module: Platform

SAP SuccessFactors introduces an AI-driven chat integration with Microsoft Teams, designed to streamline communication. The AI assists by providing contextually relevant responses and suggestions, making collaboration more efficient and user-friendly. With this feature, users can initiate quick actions and complete tasks simply by typing natural-language requests. The feature supports multiple locales and recognizes different phrases with the same intention. For example, whether users type "I want to request a day off" or "request OOO" or something else with the same meaning, the app will return the Request Time off quick action to help them proceed.

AI-Assisted Microsoft Teams App Chat, is now available in Work Tech

 

2. AI-Assisted Skill Recommendations from Continuous Performance Management

  • Module: Performance & Goals / Platform

Using data from Continuous Performance Management (CPM), Talent Intelligence Hub can now use AI-assisted capabilities to infer and recommend skills to employees in their Growth Portfolio. This ensures employees are always aware of the skills they should focus on to enhance their growth and development.

Talent Intelligence Hub can now use AI-assisted capabilities

Talent Intelligence Hub can now use AI-assisted capabilities

 

3. AI-Assisted Skills Standardization

  • Module: Platform

We can now use AI-assisted capabilities to use the industry-standard skill names in the Attributes Library. Consistency is key, and SAP SuccessFactors addresses this by leveraging AI to standardize skill names across job profiles, resumes, and talent pools. This feature minimizes redundancy and ensures uniformity in skill tracking.

TIH Skills Standardization

 

4. AI-Assisted Career Insights

  • Module: Succession & Development

In Career Development, now leveraging generative AI capabilities, employees can gain insights into development actions they can take towards their target role. AI technology into the Career Worksheet feature, can unlock a wealth of knowledge and recommendations to accelerate their career development journey.

Boost your career with AI-powered insights

 

5. AI-Assisted Writing for Message to Recipient of a Spot Award

  • Module: Compensation

When sending messages for Spot Awards, AI assists managers by generating impactful message templates. This feature not only saves time but ensures that communications are professional and motivating. When you select the AI tool after selecting any text in the Message to Recipient text box, AI-assisted writing options other than the Generate Message are available to use.

AI-assisted writing features for reward recipient message

 

6. Enhanced AI-Assisted Writing in 360 Reviews

  • Module: Performance & Goals

The 360-degree feedback process benefits from AI-driven writing assistance, enabling reviewers to provide structured and constructive comments. This is especially useful for providing comprehensive feedback with minimal effort. Two action items are now accessible from the context menu of that tool: Improve My Feedback & Generate Comments.

AI-assisted writing tool to help generate comments and improve feedback

 

7. AI-Assisted Insights into Detailed 360 Report

  • Module: Performance & Goals

The AI in 360 reviews didn't stop at writing— it also offers detailed insights into 360 review reports. By analyzing patterns and data, AI helps managers make better-informed decisions about performance and talent development. The generative AI in the Detailed 360 Report can be used in two scenarios: Overall understanding of a Detailed 360 Report & Consolidated analysis of a specific skill or competency.

Overall Insights for 360 Report

 

8. Enhanced AI-Assisted Writing in Opportunity Marketplace Assignments

  • Module: Opportunity Marketplace

In Opportunity Marketplace, assignment owners and co-owners can now create and edit assignments using generative AI capabilities. This feature generates relevant assignments based on organizational needs, allowing for optimal talent allocation. When assignment owners or co-owners use the updated Enhance Writing action, it now considers the following context in addition to the existing description text:

  • Assignment type
  • Assignment title
  • Job role defined for the assignment when job role is enabled for assignments
  • Attributes added for the assignment

AI-Assisted Writing in OMP

 

9. Enhancements to AI-Assisted Goal Creation

  • Module: Performance & Goals / Succession & Development

As you know Goal Management module features an AI-assisted goal creation tool.SAP made several enhancements to AI-assisted goal creation in the latest Goal Management. Enhancements includes:

  • Updated AI tag : "AI-generated content", replacing the previous "AI-enhanced content"
  •  Streamlined content comparison: AI-assisted writing tool is now in place to enhance content directly at the field level. Compare Generated Content page has been removed.
  • Improved prompt template: prompt template is now optimized to automatically apply goal plan configurations, such as start, due, and maxlength, to AI-generated goals
  • Support for custom fields: Custom fields of text and textarea types are now supported for performance goals. By providing meaningful text for <field-description>, you specify the context, which in turn helps improve the quality of AI-generated content.

AI-Assisted Goal Creation

 

 

10. AI-Assisted Job Skills on Career Sites

  • Module: Recruiting

Recruiting has been enhanced with AI-driven skill matching on career sites. This feature helps highlight the most relevant skills for job postings, ensuring companies attract the right candidate. With this enhancement, you can now add a Skills Cloud component to the Job layout in Career Site Builder, so that job skills are displayed on your career site in the form of a word cloud.

AI-Assisted Skills Cloud

 

11. AI-Assisted Skills Matching for Candidate Resumes

  • Module: Recruiting

AI also plays a crucial role in resume screening by matching candidates' resumes with job requisitions. This speeds up the recruitment process and ensures that only the best-fit candidates move forward. When the AI-Assisted Skills Matching for Resumes feature is enabled, the search results page on career sites displays the option to upload a resume. You have the option to present the candidate with a data privacy consent statement (DPCS) before they upload it. After they accept the DPCS and upload their resume, we use AI to analyze the resume and identify their skills. Then, we recommend jobs that match their skills.

AI-Assisted Skills Matching for Resumes

 

12. AI-Assisted Skills Validation for Job Requisitions

  • Module: Recruiting

Before job postings go live, AI ensures that all required skills are validated. This prevents mismatches between job requirements and candidate qualifications, making the recruitment process more efficient. Recruiters can perform the following tasks to enhance the skills listed in the job requirements:

  • Add, remove, and save skills in the job requisition to control the visibility of the skills listed in the job posting on the career site.
  • Categorize primary and secondary skills to find candidates who best fit the job based on their skills matching the skills specified in the job requirements.

Skills Validation for Job Requisitions

 

13. Enhanced AI-Assisted Writing Across Modules

  • Module: Across Suite

The Text Analyzer, a new AI-enhanced feature, brings advanced capabilities such as translating text and analyzing for biases. It scans for potential age, gender, and cultural biases, ensuring inclusivity and fairness in communications .

The following modules are adopting AI-assisted writing for the first time in the 2H 2024 release:

  • Compensation
  • Rewards and Recognition
  • Variable Pay
  • Calibration
  • Employee Central
  • Platform
  • Learning
  • Onboarding
  • Dynamic Teams (including Objectives and Key Results)
  • Latest People Profile
  • Talent Intelligence Hub

Text Analyzer scans for the following safety attributes like age bias, gender bias, etc.

AI-Assisted Writing & Translations

 

14. AI in Learning: Use AI to Associate Skills With Learning Items

  • Module: Learning

The Learning module benefits from AI, which associates relevant skills with learning content automatically. This ensures employees are always learning the right skills for their roles. You can use the AI-assisted capabilities to associate skills with Open Content Network (OCN) items based on the predefined skills provided by the OCN providers and thru imports for all learning items.

Learning Items - Skills Inference

 

 

This comprehensive list of AI features captures the essence of the 2H 2024 release and its significant impact on HR management. These innovations making sure that employees and managers can focus on what truly matters: growth, development, and success. AI isn’t just changing how we work—it’s making HR more human.

Are you ready to embrace the future of work?

Driving HR Innovation with Artificial Intelligence

entry.title: Vector Indexing for Retrieval-Augmented Generation with Databricks, SAP HANA, and Generative AI Hub
entry.content:
Introduction

In the rapidly evolving landscape of artificial intelligence, the combination of vector indexing and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) has emerged as a powerful approach for enhancing the accuracy and relevance of generated content. By leveraging the capabilities of Databricks, SAP HANA Database, and tools for Generative AI like the Generative AI Hub SDK and LangChain, we can create a robust framework for efficient and scalable vector indexing.
In my previous blog post, we saw how LangChain could be used with SAP HANA for vector indexing and RAG. In this blog post, we will explore the step-by-step process of setting up a vector indexing pipeline using Databricks for data processing, SAP HANA as the vector database, and the Generative AI Hub SDK alongside LangChain for seamless integration. At each step, code examples for a Databricks notebook will be provided. Whether you're a data scientist, AI researcher, or a developer looking to enhance your RAG workflows, this guide will provide you with the insights and practical knowledge needed to get started. Let's dive into the intricacies of this powerful combination and unlock new potentials for your AI-driven projects.

Setting Up Our Environment

Databricks clusters come with a good number of pre-installed packages for data science, data engineering and AI purposes, so tools like Spark and LangChain are already available by default. However, there are some packages that we will need for the example pipeline that we will be building:
  • html2text: For converting text from HTML to Markdown format
  • generative-ai-hub-sdk: For working with Generative AI models in the Generative AI Hub
  • hdbcli: For connection to a HANA Database

The following code snippet could be executed in Databricks to install these packages:

 

%pip install html2text "generative-ai-hub-sdk[all]" hdbcli

dbutils.library.restartPython()

 

 

Note: While generative-ai-hub-sdk and hdbcli are required for the vector indexing process, html2text is only required for this example. Your use cases might not necessarily require html2text, but might require other packages instead, so you should adjust the pip install command accordingly to fit your use cases.

Building Our Vector Indexing Pipeline

Building a vector indexing pipeline is essentially a data engineering task, and as such, we could split the pipeline processes into the Extract, Transform, and Load (ETL) steps.

Extract

The extract step involves ingesting raw data from one or more data sources. For our example, our raw data are HTML files exported from an Atlassian Confluence wiki space, where each HTML file contains the contents of a single wiki page. These files are stored in a container within an Azure Data Lake Service (ADLS) account, and can be read using PySpark as shown:

 

from pyspark.sql.functions import expr

HTML_FILEPATHS_GLOB_PATTERN = "abfss://aitm@coredatalaketestint.dfs.core.windows.net/sandbox/example_wikis/*"

html_binary_spark_df = (
    spark
    .read
    .format("binaryFile")
    .options(pathGlobFilter="*.html")
    .load(HTML_FILEPATHS_GLOB_PATTERN)
    .withColumn("content", expr("CAST(content AS STRING)"))
)

 

 

If the extract step goes well, we should get a PySpark DataFrame containing these columns:
  • path: the full paths to the HTML files, which look something like abfss://aitm@coredatalaketestint.dfs.core.windows.net/sandbox/example_wikis/GAI01.03.10-RAGe_4337208805.html
  • modificationTime: timestamp values indicating when the files were last modified
  • length: length of the file contents
  • content: decoded HTML contents of the files

 

Note:
  • The snippet above shows how we can ingest data that exists as raw binary files in ADLS, but your use cases might involve data in different formats (e.g. CSV, Parquet) or from different sources (e.g. database, API), so you will need to adjust your ingestion logic accordingly. The documentation for working with inputs and outputs using PySpark is a good place to figure out how you could use Spark for ingesting your data.
  • In this example, HTML_FILEPATHS_GLOB_PATTERN points to a container in ADLS where the raw data is stored, with aitm being the container name, coredatalaketestint.dfs.core.windows.net being the address of the ADLS instance, and sandbox/example_wikis/ being the folder within the aitm container where the data is stored. For different cloud platforms (GCP, AWS etc), the URI would be different, so it is advised to check the relevant documentations from Spark or the respective cloud platforms.

Transform

After we have ingested our data, the next step is to clean and preprocess our data into a suitable format for vector indexing. For this example, we will perform the following to preprocess our data:
  • Converting the contents from HTML to Markdown: Markdown is syntactically simpler compared to HTML, which makes it more understandable to Large Language Models (LLMs), thus we do this conversion
  • Removing unnecessary contents: We will remove headers and footers in page contents to reduce noise in the data, which could help improve the retrieval performance of a RAG application. We also remove authors from the contents for privacy reasons.
  • Getting the base file names: This is an optional step to get the base file names from the path values in the path column, for recording as metadata during the indexing step later.
These smaller steps can be done as shown:

 

import re
import html2text
from pyspark.sql.functions import udf, col
from pyspark.sql.types import StringType

AUTHORS_REGEX_PATTERN = re.compile(r"Created\sby.*last\smodified.*[A-Z][a-z]{2}\s\d{2}\,\s\d{4}")

convert_html_to_markdown = udf(html2text.html2text, StringType())

@udf(StringType())
def strip_header_and_footer(content: str) -> str:
    return content[53:-100]

@udf(StringType())
def remove_authors(content: str) -> str:
    return AUTHORS_REGEX_PATTERN.sub("", content)

@udf(StringType())
def get_filename(path: str) -> str:
    return path.split("/")[-1]

wiki_texts_spark_df = (
    html_binary_spark_df
    .withColumn("content", convert_html_to_markdown(col("content")))
    .withColumn("content", strip_header_and_footer(col("content")))
    .withColumn("content", remove_authors(col("content")))
    .withColumn("filename", get_filename(col("path")))
    .selectExpr("filename", "content")
)

 

 

If the cleaning and preprocessing steps go well, we should get a different PySpark DataFrame containing these columns:
  • filename: the base name of the HTML files, which look something like GAI01.03.10-RAGe_4337208805.html
  • content: the cleaned contents of the wiki pages in Markdown format

 

Note: Spark user-defined functions (UDFs) can be used to apply preprocessing steps to the columns of a PySpark DataFrame in a simple and clean fashion. More information about PySpark UDFs can be found in the PySpark documentation.
After cleaning our data, we will still need to split our wiki contents into chunks. This is especially important if we are working with LLMs that have lower token limits. We also typically want to have smaller chunks of text to reduce the risk of encountering the lost-in-the-middle problem during the RAG process.
For our example, we use LangChain to load the PySpark DataFrame and perform the chunking of wiki contents:

 

from langchain_community.document_loaders import PySparkDataFrameLoader
from langchain_text_splitters import RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter

full_contents_loader = PySparkDataFrameLoader(spark_session=spark, df=wiki_texts_spark_df, page_content_column="content")
documents_to_split = full_contents_loader.load()

document_splitter = RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter.from_tiktoken_encoder(
    model_name="gpt-4",
    chunk_size=200,
    chunk_overlap=20
)

document_chunks = document_splitter.split_documents(documents_to_split)

 

 

Note: While we use the RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter class from LangChain with chunk_size of 200 and chunk_overlap of 20 as an example, other chunking strategies might work better for your own use cases. Therefore, some exploration should be done to determine chunking strategies that work better for your use cases. For a starting place on chunking strategy, you can refer to this Medium post.

Load

Now that we have cleaned and preprocessed our data, we are ready for the indexing step itself.
As we are using an embedding model from the Generative AI Hub as well as the HANA Database for the vector indexing step, we need to load the secrets for these services. For this example, these secrets have been stored as Databricks secrets which can be loaded as shown:

 

import json

SECRET_SCOPE = "TEST_AITM_SCOPE"
gen_ai_hub_service_key = json.loads(dbutils.secrets.get(scope=SECRET_SCOPE, key="EXAMPLE_GENAI_HUB_SERVICE_KEY"))
hana_secrets = json.loads(dbutils.secrets.get(scope=SECRET_SCOPE, key="EXAMPLE_HANA_VECTOR_SECRETS"))

 

After loading the secrets, we want to initialize the client objects for Generative AI Hub and HANA Database respectively as shown:

 

# HANA DB
from hdbcli import dbapi

hana_conn = dbapi.connect(
    address=hana_secrets["host"],
    port=hana_secrets["port"],
    user=hana_secrets["user"],
    password=hana_secrets["password"],
    autocommit=True,
    sslTrustStore=hana_secrets["certificate"],
)


# GenAI Hub
import os
from gen_ai_hub.proxy.core.proxy_clients import get_proxy_client

os.environ["AICORE_AUTH_URL"] = gen_ai_hub_service_key["url"]
os.environ["AICORE_CLIENT_ID"] = gen_ai_hub_service_key["clientid"]
os.environ["AICORE_CLIENT_SECRET"] = gen_ai_hub_service_key["clientsecret"]
os.environ["AICORE_RESOURCE_GROUP"] = gen_ai_hub_service_key["appname"].split("!")[0]
os.environ["AICORE_BASE_URL"] = f"{gen_ai_hub_service_key['serviceurls']['AI_API_URL']}/v2"

proxy_client = get_proxy_client("gen-ai-hub")

 

We then want to initialize the LangChain objects for working with our embedding model and HANA DB:

 

from gen_ai_hub.proxy.langchain.init_models import init_embedding_model
from langchain_community.vectorstores import HanaDB

embeddings = init_embedding_model("text-embedding-ada-002", proxy_client=proxy_client)
hana_vectordb = HanaDB(embedding=embeddings, connection=hana_conn, table_name="DATABRICKS_HANA_EXAMPLE_VECTORSTORE")

 

Note: My previous blog post gives more explanation of this code snippet, so you may want to check it out.

The last bit of the indexing process is a simple method call to write the chunks to HANA DB. LangChain will take care of the embedding of chunks and writing to HANA under the hood.

 

hana_vectordb.add_documents(document_chunks)

 

Optionally, we can run the following code snippet to test the retrieval of document chunks, as a sanity check:

 

hana_vector_retriever = hana_vectordb.as_retriever()

hana_vector_retriever.get_relevant_documents("What is RAG?")

 

 

What Next?

Now that we have our pipeline notebook, the typical next step would be to make our pipeline scalable by creating a Databricks job that runs that notebook. Configurations such as trigger schedules or notifications depend on the use case and can be set in Databricks accordingly. More information can be found in the Databricks documentation for jobs.
The vectors that are indexed can be consumed by a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) application to enhance the relevance and accuracy of generated content. For an example of how to consume these vectors using LangChain, refer to my previous blog post where I demonstrate the integration and utilization of these vectors in a RAG workflow.

Conclusion

In this blog post, we've delved into the intricate process of setting up a vector indexing pipeline using Databricks, SAP HANA, and Generative AI tools like the Generative AI Hub SDK and LangChain. By carefully walking through each step—from data extraction and transformation to loading and indexing—we've demonstrated how to build a scalable and efficient pipeline that significantly enhances the accuracy and relevance of AI-generated content. This powerful combination of technologies not only streamlines your RAG workflows but also opens new avenues for innovation in AI-driven projects.  Feel free to leave your comments and share your thoughts or questions about this blog post.
Happy coding and may your AI projects reach new heights of excellence! 🥂

entry.title: Lessons from the SAP Advisory Council for Waste & Recycling annual meeting
entry.content:
On September 25th  and 26th took place in Walldorf the annual presential meeting  of the SAP Advisory Council for Waste and Recycling is a,  by invitation only, a select group of companies  willing to collaborate in an open environment with SAP. With more of 25 editions, the Advisory Council is a well established forum whose members drive their companies digital vision and stablish a community beyond the physical meetings with the following purposes.

​- Feedback and validation

​- Collaboration

​- Communication

​- Product adoption information exchange

 ​Image:  SAP Advisory Council for Waste & Recycling meeting atendees

​This year edition attended more than 25 delegates from 9 companies plus  Prologa, in the condition of developer of strategic industry cloud solutions for the Waste and Recycling industry and Gruler & Cleve Consulting  which accepted the invitation to share their experiences and help conducting a design thinking workshop.

Agenda for this year was divided in three parts:  product strategy and development updates, ideation workshop on Artificial Intelligence and conversion experiences into S/4HANA.

​SAP  product strategy and development updates were presented by Solution Management and Product Management teams (Image:  Uwe Zachmann and Somik Sarkar, Waste industry responsibles from Product Management team) which offered their respective perspectives, this is from extending the scope of current solution into the recycling part of the value chain to a detailed description and demonstration of the new developments released for the SAP for Waste & Recycling  part of S/4HANA plus insights of the ongoing and future developments.  All these updates provided were explained in the context of the overall SAP product portfolio strategy around RISE offering, where Artificial Intelligence capabilities will play a key role.  In this sense, and as an introduction for the later comprehensive ideation session on this subject, a PoC of Jule showing a use case for the waste collection process was shown.

​Prologa presented very interested and relevant updates as well.  Of special relevance has been the introduction of Mr. Christoph Hillmann as Prologa VP for Circular Economy & CoDevelopment who made a vibrant presentation of their product strategy and the ongoing development roadmap for their components, with special focus in the Order Planning and Dispatching solution plus a spark of their updated go – to – market strategy which will include an event next november   

 

​Image:  Ideation session

​Second part of the meeting has been an ideation workshop on AI can help to develop waste & recycling business. Session was brillianty conducted by Isabel Creuznacher from Gruler & Cleve Consulting jointly with Henán Sendra from the Center of Expertise - CoE of SAP BTP.

​ Part one

  • Short Intro of objective WS
  • Inspiration overview:  AI for SAP
  • Intro Design Thinking as Method for WS

​Part two

  • Teambuilding
  • Exercise Needfinding Journey (participants):  discuss the journey and define the problems
  • Group presentations main pain points (participants)

 

​Part three

  • Split teams according to problem area
  • Intro Ideation Session
  • Ideation Sessions for use cases

oBrainstorming / “crazy 8” (individual)

oSelect for the best 10 ideas (group)

  • Selection of idea (ideation matrix for feasibility)
  • Wrap-up

​Third part of the meeting was dedicated to the conversion experiences of SAP ECC systems into S4HANA.  A very big thank you to our co-chairs Roman Aiyer from WBD - Duisburg and Matthias Reich from Prezero for the great feedback on their S4HANA conversion projects with the great help of Mr. Jürgen Krist.

​.We are confident that more successful and interesting sessions will come in the future. Noticing that members play an active part in the orchestration and conduction of activities is a positive signal.  For all involved SAP departments, the value of the Advisory Council is undisputed.

 

​

entry.title: [sunset] Business Entity Recognition is getting deprecated 02/25
entry.content:
Revisiting Business Entity Recognition (BER):

As part of the SAP AI Services portfolio, Business Entity Recognition was introduced with a clear goal: to improve text document processing by detecting and highlighting any type of named entity in unstructured text and sorting into predefined categories. This was achieved using advanced artificial intelligence and natural language processing technologies.
Key features included:
  • Extract Text Entity - Pre-trained Model: Customers could use pre-trained machine learning models to detect and highlight, in unstructured text, business entities that belong to predefined categories.
  • Extract Text Entity - Custom Model: Customers could create their own custom machine learning models to detect and highlight any type of business entity in unstructured text.

How Business Entity Recognition (BER) Helped Customers:

Since its release, the BER has helped customers simplify their text document processing using numerous ways. Here are some ways it contributed:
  • Increased efficiency ​and reduced occurrence of errors
  • Increased productivity and quick time-to-value with pre-trained models
  • Increased scalability of document processing operations without proportional increases in resource costs.

Looking Ahead: Integration with Document Information Extraction:

While the sun is setting for BER, its core capabilities will live on and evolve. We are excited to announce that key features of Business Entity Recognition will be integrated into Document Information Extraction. These features will be available in the first half of 2025 and will offer a seamless and enriched user experience.
 
We thank our customers for their trust in Business Entity Recognition and look forward to ushering in a new era of innovation and excellence together with the enhanced Document Information Extraction. Stay tuned for more updates as we constantly evolve the roadmap for the Document Information Extraction. For existing customers, we remain committed to supporting BER till the end of their contract.
 
#documentinformationextraction 
@tomasz_janasz

entry.title: Where can I start learning how to use SAP's generative AI Hub to solve your business problems?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Solving Your Business Problems Using Prompts and LLMs in SAP's Generative AI Hub learning journey

Overview
At the end of this learning journey, you will be able to use SAP's generative AI Hub to solve your business problems using LLMs, SAP AI Launchpad, and generative-ai-hub SDK. 
You will be able to apply techniques for refining and evaluating prompts using different large language models in generative AI hub.

Learning objectives

  • Describe AI foundation and generative AI hub 
  • Learn how to access SAP's generative AI hub.
  • Identify the need of using SDKs for LLMs.
  • Describe SAP's Generative AI hub SDK.
  • Explain  advanced prompting methods.
  • Describe different models in generative AI hub.
  • Configure different models in generative AI hub.
  • Explain generative AI applications beyond chatbots, using software-driven input and output processing scenarios.

Prerequisites

  • Navigating Large Language Models fundamentals and techniques for your use case

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area.

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Unlock the Power of SAP HCM GenAI: Discover Use Cases, Pre-requisites, and RBP Requirements
entry.content:
There's a lot of chatter about SAP Business AI and how it's shaking up the HRTech space, especially when it comes to HCM AI use cases. If you're headed to SuccessConnect Lisbon—or tuning in virtually—trust me, you don’t want to miss some of the awesome sessions lined up! But before you get there, let me share a sneak peek of what we're cooking up in the world of HCM AI.

So, what is SAP Business AI exactly?

In a nutshell, it's our way of making business apps a whole lot smarter by sprinkling in AI magic. Think of it like this: Joule, our AI copilot, doesn’t just understand commands—it actually gets your business. And that’s not all. We’ve also got AI services tailored to specific business processes built on our BTP platform, like document processing, data recommendations, and even robotic process automation (RPA)—just to name a few.

 And we're not doing this alone! We're teaming up with some big players in the industry. In some cases, we’re embedding their tech right into our products—like what we’re doing with Microsoft. In others, we’re making it easy for our customers to build their own custom ML models using SAP data—securely, of course.

Let’s talk about types of AI use cases in SuccessFactors

and why SAP Business AI is your secret sauce for a smarter HR strategy.

First up, Relevant Integration: our AI is embedded right into your HCM data, so when it generates a job description, it’s tailored to the actual skills you need—not a cookie-cutter list. Next, Reliable Expertise: we’re not just tossing AI into the mix and hoping for the best. Our prompts are carefully crafted, tested at scale, and designed to give you the most accurate responses every time. Lastly, Responsible AI: We take ethics, data privacy, and bias seriously. Plus, we’ve got hyperscaler agreements to keep everything on the up and up. Because, let’s face it, AI without responsibility is like a car with no brakes—it's not going to end well.

Here’s a breakdown of HCM AI types available with the SuccessFactors suite:

 To summarize, here's an overview of the key AI use cases within SuccessFactors Suite. We will focus on GenAI HCM Use cases in this Blog.

Process to enable HCM GenAI Features

 

How do you enable GenAI use cases in your instance? There's plenty of info scattered across the PDC and customer community, but let me give you a quick, high-level overview to simplify things!!

 After purchasing AI units (step #2), the Ops Provisioning team activates AI services on their end, which then enables two switches in SuccessFactors Provisioning (step #3):

  • Enable AI-assisted HCM Content Generator
  • Enable AI-Assisted HXM Skills

    NOTE: Once these switches are activated, Admins will be able to see the 'AI Services Administration' role in Role-Based Permissions (RBP). If, for any reason, you're unable to see the AI services transactions after purchasing AI units, you can reach out to SF_Provisioning@sap.com for assistance.

    A key point to remember, users leveraging any GenAI feature must have RBP access to the relevant module.

    SAP Business AI Use Cases for HCM

    Here's a list of Premium AI use cases in SuccessFactors.

    Link to: Premium AI Features in SAP SuccessFactors

    Use CaseSuccessFactors Solution Pre-requisites

    Joule Actions

    Dependent on underlying actions – documented here

    See Guide to Activate Joule in SuccessFactors

      • Joule Order, i.e., Joule SKU (no cost SKU)
      • BTP Global Account & Subaccount for Joule provisioning
      • SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition for navigation service
      • SAP Cloud Identity Services (IAS & IPS)

    Joule Assisted Q&A Capability

    See Document Grounding L0 Deck & SAP AI Services List

      • Steps as above with Joule Actions
      • Document Grounding AI Service
      • Document Specifications
        • Data Repository - MS SharePoint | Doc Formats - PDF and MS Word
        • Content - plain text | Language - English only

    AI Person Insights for Compensation Discussions

    SuccessFactors Compensation, SuccessFactors Employee Central

    AI Assisted Job Descriptions (Enhancing Job Descriptions with AI)

    SuccessFactors Recruiting (note: this feature works for customers using JDM or JPB)

    AI Assisted Interview Questions with MS Teams

    SuccessFactors Recruiting, MS Teams, SuccessFactors App for Microsoft Teams

    AI Assisted Goals Generation (Performance Goals and Development Goals

    Performance Management, Goals Management

    AI Assisted Skills Matching for Applicant Screening

    SuccessFactors Recruiting

    AI Assisted Skills Architecture Creation

    Job Profile Builder, Talent Intelligence Hub

    AI Assisted Writing

    Relevant SuccessFactors modules (available in select modules today & availability will expand through 2025).

     

Here's a list of key configuration steps and RBP permissions for each of these use cases. I hope this information is helpful.

 

 

As always, pulling together these product details is a team effort—it truly takes a village! Huge thanks to my amazing colleagues Robert Wood and Davis Dudchock, our Product leads for HCM Business AI. If you're looking for more insights on SAP HCM Business AI use cases or Joule, join us at SuccessConnect in Lisbon Ask SAP experts: AI for HR (ASK100). You won’t want to miss it!

If you are unable to attend SC Lisbon, you can attend SuccessConnect Virtual, and there are many AI-related sessions. Registration for the virtual event is free.

entry.title: SAP’s Generative AI approach to create Vodafone’s AI Finance Advocate
entry.content:
The partnership between Vodafone and SAP has been a significant one in the telecom industry directly touching more than 300 million users.  Since Vodafone’s migration to SAP S/4 HANA, Vodafone has been looking for innovative ways to tackle its business challenges.  One such challenge came from the Finance Operations department, which is managing Lease Accounting and asset restoration obligations (ARO). 

The Business Challenge 

In financial accounting, there are large volumes of policies including international standards like International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS) to guide the handling of the various scenarios.  How assets are treated in accounting could depend on numerous factors like the type of asset, the location of the asset, the local tax guidelines, international standards, restoration procedures, lease terms, and inflation.  The effort required to train employees on all these complex policies and to keep them up to date with changes is huge. Employees need constant support for accounting queries, which is causing a strain on the global support team.  The human-operated resolution is causing delays in the posting in the SAP S/4HANA system, and the resulting operation is expensive. 

Generative AI Solution 

 This unique challenge of guiding the Vodafone Finance team in accurate accounting practices forced a unique generative GenAI approach and a Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) application on SAP BTP was devised.  A Finance Advocate with the capability to interact and answer users’ queries in natural language was built.  This GenAI powered tool was trained on the latest policy, process, and training documents with the ability to answer all types of questions ranging from simple principle-based queries to complex scenario-based ones. All internal input documents and query data are kept secure within the Vodafone environment without any exposure to the public.  This internal data is combined with public policy documents like IFRS to enrich the knowledge of the tool. The knowledge base of this digital tool is maintained by an administrator who can update or append the latest version policies. 

Impact and Outcome  

The Finance Advocate will act as a tool to the Lease Accounting and ARO teams clarifying their queries on policies instantly.  The 24/7 and quick response from the Finance Advocate tool will enable accountants to take appropriate actions directly. This will increase the quality of the S/4 postings and save the global support team from spending significant hours of effort handling ticket after ticket. The support team can direct their time to more important and innovative tasks.   

 The Finance Advocate’s knowledge can be further enhanced by feeding it with internal notes from previous queries.  That historical data can improve the quality of the generated responses and share the learnings across the team. 

 The Finance Advocate smoothly supports the onboarding of new employees at a quicker pace and minimizes the overhead of training costs.  

 Overall, this will lead to a more accurate, robust, and consistent accounting treatment, to ensure reliable financial statements and regulatory compliance. 

 Looking ahead, Vodafone intends to expand its use of the open and flexible GenAI Hub and HANA Cloud Vector Engine services to support additional finance use cases like finance process compliance and the preparation of statutory reporting, including the integration with SAP S/4HANA. 

System Architecture 

 The overall data flow follows SAP’s suggested RAG reference architecture and the application is based on SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP). The Vodafone administrator will log in to the solution securely and load all the knowledge documents. This can be redone when there are new documents or updates to older documents. Following this knowledge ingestion process, the application will be ready to be used by the end user. The end user will be authorized upon login and be able to post queries in natural language. 

The core of the solution is the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP) application which controls the flow of the data from the SAP HANA Cloud vector engine to the GenAI Hub, through which it connects to the partner foundational LLMs. The implementation of the answer generation and chat completion within the CAP application are simplified by the use of wrapper methods provided by the CAP LLM Plugin. The security of data across all exchange channels (LLM included) is ensured by appropriate access controls and protocol adherence. None of the company-specific sensitive data can be used by the partner LLMs for their further training of fine-tuning. 

Design Elements 

In the user interface, the elements were built with basic controls to keep the interface simple and easy to use. The user can first choose the LLM model which will be responsible for answering the query and then enter the query. In the user interface, the left panel provides access to the chat history. The right panel provides per query the details with information linking to the original source document from where the query answer is derived and its sub-section. 

Features and Highlights 

Multi-model Solution 

Securely Integrated

Extensible Design  

The solution provides flexibility for the user to select the LLM model.  The default options are Azure OpenAI GPT4, Claude 3 via Amazon Bedrock & Google Gemini Pro, but can be extended to include more models.  The user’s preferences, the model’s performance, and the nature of the current question should influence the user’s choice. 

The SAP BTP hosted solution follows the standard security protocols and authorizes the users by principal propagation.  The access rights of the users to specific functionalities like data access from SAP S/4HANA are honored.  The user roles in the SAP BTP control access to the admin and the end-user screens by establishing a trust. 

The design of the application allows for easy extensions and integrations with other use cases and future requirements.  The knowledge base of the application is expandable.  The GenAI solution can be further enhanced with agents for any scenario specific requirements like mathematical calculations and accounting insights. 

Collaborative Development Process between SAP and Vodafone 

From the initial stages of requirement identification through design discussions, implementation, and final deployment, the development of the AI Finance Advocate has exemplified a strong and productive collaboration between SAP and Vodafone. The journey began as a co-innovation effort, with both teams working closely to enhance the consistency and accuracy of the tool. 

The project was developed incrementally, with the initial version hosted in an SAP Playground environment. This setup facilitated seamless collaboration, allowing both teams to iteratively test and refine the solution. As the project matured, it underwent a rigorous review and approval process by Vodafone's security team. Following this approval, the AI Finance Advocate was successfully ported and deployed into Vodafone’s infrastructure. Finance Business domain experts were then engaged to conduct comprehensive acceptance testing, ensuring the solution met all requirements. 

This collaborative effort and mutual support between SAP and Vodafone stand as a testament to the strength of the partnership and the shared commitment to innovation. 

Summary 

This AI Finance Advocate is enabling Vodafone to manage the Lease Accounting and ARO scenarios efficiently and thus to increase the productivity and improve the quality of the financial statements.    Vodafone will be able to easily ingest additional financial guidelines as well as to upskill the Finance team thanks to this intelligent GenAI Hub solution. 

Does your business have a trove of guidelines, manuals or training and process documents that employees must reference every day?  Does your business have other challenges that could be addressed with a generative AI solution?  Hopefully, this solution and blog post will fuel your imagination and inspire ideas. If you need more information about this use case or Generative AI at SAP, please contact our team paa@SAP.com to find the best possible solution. 

Credits 

A big thanks to Manuela Godeck, Shubam Muthiyan, Balazs Sebestyen and Abhishek Paul from Vodafone for collaborating with us throughout this journey.  Thanks to the SAP colleagues for their contributions: Akash Amarendra, Feng Liang, Mark Hillier and Paul Everett.  And special thanks to Sivakumar N for his guidance and suggestions.

entry.title: SAP TechEd Keynote 2024 – Technology Highlights
entry.content:
SAP TechEd 2024 kicked off virtually, bringing you a lot of new exciting technology updates. In this highlights blog post, I have compiled a quick summary of the announcements and updates shared by Michael Ameling, Chief Product Officer, SAP Business Technology Platform, to help you navigate everything that is new around SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP).

BTP General:

Let’s start with some of the general updates on SAP BTP.

First and foremost, SAP is expanding BTP Coverage in the next years via eight SAP-managed data centers.

I am sure, you are all aware of SAP Discovery Center is the central place to go for businesses looking to adopt SAP’s cloud solutions efficiently with the help of multiple mission-based learnings. These missions are structured guides that walk users through specific use cases and help users achieve business goals using BTP services.

SAP now announced for the SAP Discovery Center the option to deploy some of the missions with a Quick-Account-Setup, helping users to get the exact technical BTP set-up as per the mission at the click of a button. Check out the quick demo below for more insights.

Quick Account Set-up 

For expert technical users, who would like to customize these missions, you will get the ready Terraform based automation scripts, which are behind QAS, publicly available here - https://github.com/SAP-samples/btp-terraform-samples/tree/main/released/discovery_center 

Application Development and Automation:

As an SAP Cloud ERP customer, it’s paramount to follow a “Clean Core” strategy to be able to receive new innovations from SAP quickly. SAP is strengthening its SAP Build portfolio to support you when building extensions, ensuring your system stays consistent, maintainable, and upgradeable.

SAP Build unifies the entire development and extension capabilities and will now also include ABAP Cloud. With that, SAP Build becomes THE extension solution for all your needs.

By bringing ABAP cloud capabilities into SAP Build, you will also be able to create and maintain ABAP Cloud projects directly from the SAP Build lobby. Watch this short demo to see SAP Build lobby in action.

SAP Build in Action 

For SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition, SAP announced the SAP Build Extensibility Wizard which helps both, key users and developers create both on-stack and side-by-side extensions in an embedded experience.

Watch this short demo to get some insights.

Custom Field creation via Joule in SAP S/4HANA 

Data and Analytics:

I am sure you remember one of SAP’s biggest announcements from SAP TechEd 2023, which was the SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine.

Since last year, SAP has made HANA Cloud Vector Engine a thousand times faster. Now SAP is adding a native in-database vectorization with its own SAP-specific text embedding model, which simplifies the creation of vector embeddings significantly.

Now SAP announced SAP Knowledge graph and SAP HANA Cloud Knowledge graph engine, which allows customers to build business and intelligent data applications like never before.

By combining the vector engine and the knowledge graph engine, you can harness the power of structured and unstructured data together.

For example, in areas like Supply Chain Management, the Knowledge Graph Engine can help you identify suppliers based on their carbon footprint. But the real value is: 

You can now factor-in any criteria that might impact your supplier selection. These could be geopolitical risks or weather conditions. The Knowledge Graph engine can give you a holistic view across existing data silos.

Both, the vector engine as well as the knowledge graph engine, are fully integrated with other existing HANA Cloud multi-model data processing capabilities.  

Stay tuned to learn more as both SAP Knowledge graph and SAP HANA Cloud Knowledge graph engine will be available in Q1 2025

SAP is making it easier for everyone to create intelligent data applications by introducing Generative AI toolkit for SAP HANA Cloud which is available via SAP Build. It allows anyone to perform common data analysis steps and handle complex machine learning operations within SAP Build code also with the help of our AI co-pilot Joule. GenAI Toolkit for SAP HANA Cloud will be available from Q1 2025.

Check out this demo to see it in action

SAP HANA Cloud Generative AI Toolkit in Action 

SAP also announced new data lake capabilities for SAP Datasphere, which allows any organization to implement business data fabric in a simple, cost-efficient and scalable manner. The new data lake capabilities are available from Q4 2024.

And: SAP is making the integration of analytics easier for you.

With the introduction of new CDS annotations, you can now use the SAP Cloud Application Programming (CAP) model to directly embed advanced analytics into your transactional applications on BTP.

SAP also introduced SAP Analytics Cloud compass. With that, you can model complex risk scenarios through the Monte Carlo simulation method. The business user friendly interface allows you to perform real-time analysis without prior setup or knowledge.

With technology continuously evolving, SAP has also brought SAP Analytics Cloud to Apple Vision Pro. See some impressions here:

SAP Analytics Cloud in Apple Vision Pro 

Integration:

SAP Integration Suite offers hundreds of connectors, thousands of pre-built integrations and embedded AI to support ALL your integration scenarios.

Early this year, SAP announced the Generative AI-based Integration Flow generation. Now, to complement that, SAP previewed the new AI capability which allows customers to optimize their running Integration Suite custom scripts in areas like memory.

Using the “Inspect” feature of SAP Integration Suite, administrators can identify the need for optimization and generate proposals including entire groovy script without writing a single line of code. See how it works in this short demo:

SAP Integration Suite AI-Script Optimization 

This feature will be available as part of the SAP Integration Suite, premium edition, in H1 2025.

SAP also announced the introduction of new Integration adapters for Anaplan, Coupa, HubSpot, NetSuite ERP, Snowflake and many more already available from Q4 2024.

And to support more mid-sized customers, SAP announced the introduction of a new license for SAP SuccessFactors and SAP GROW customers: The SAP integration Suite, starter edition, available from Q4 2024.

Artificial Intelligence:

No technology keynote is complete today without talking about AI. So, here we go.

SAP is integrating AI into your everyday work through the AI Foundation on SAP Business Technology Platform.

Customers can leverage the broadest selection of foundational models from top providers all in one place through the Generative AI Hub with the benefit of one unified legal and commercial framework.

While Joule is getting integrated into multiple SAP solutions, we are making Joule even more flexible and customizable with the introduction of Joule studio in SAP Build.

Joule studio allows partners and customers to create and deploy custom Joule skills.

And that’s not all: Joule also goes agentic. With an in-built agentic capability, Joule can now solve complex business problems for you through planning, self-reflection, logical reasoning and collaboration. Learn more about it here:

Joule goes Agentic 

Joule now brings ABAP developer capabilities inside of ABAP in Eclipse, based on SAP’s own ABAP Large Language Model (LLM). ABAP LLM has been trained on over 250 million lines of code from SAP S/4HANA Cloud. It’s available now for selected customers and partners in an early adopter version and will be generally available in Q1 2025.

While Joule is just only one year old, SAP will already have infused 80% of the most used workflows in Joule by the end of this year. SAP also announced Joule for mobile to support you while you are on the move, which will be available in SAP Start during Q4 2024 and in other applications early next year.

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

While this is only a summary of the key technology highlights, I would encourage you to watch the full keynote here: https://www.sap.com/events/teched/virtual.html

And to learn more about all the new innovations and product updates, check out our TechEd News Guide:

https://www.sap.com/events/teched/news-guide.html  

Have fun at SAP TechEd 2024!

entry.title: Kickstart SAP TechEd 2024 with Autonomous Finance and Maximize Your Business Efficiency
entry.content:
SAP TechEd 2024 is here! As we kick off for this pivotal event in the SAP calendar, anticipation is building around the revolutionary talks and groundbreaking ideas set to be unveiled. For this year’s fully virtual TechEd, I’m excited to share the new SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) use case, Autonomous Finance.

 

What is Autonomous Finance?

So, what exactly is autonomous finance, and why is it becoming a game-changer in the world of oCFO? Gartner states that autonomous finance is a “finance function where processes and activities are partly governed and majority operated by self-learning software agents that optimize front-, middle- and back-office operations." Powered by technologies like AI, machine learning, and automation, it will affect all parts of the finance function under the office of the CFO. 

 

So why should businesses be excited about autonomous finance? Let's examine some of its compelling advantages.

  1. Autonomy in finance drastically reduces human intervention, which translates into significant cost reductions for organizations.
  2. It also speeds up operations and boosts productivity.
  3. AI-driven analytics can provide deep, actionable insights from financial data, empowering businesses to make smarter, strategic decisions.

Take a look at this infographic on the trusted path to autonomous finance. 

 

Customer Success Stories

Several SAP customers are already tapping into the potential of Autonomous Finance to propel their businesses forward:

  • Connect departments: Endress+Hauser significantly boosted overall efficiency and simplified operations by utilizing SAP Integration Suite’s tools to standardize their data sources. Thanks to the suite’s APIs and capabilities, Endress+Hauser's customers can now effortlessly place equipment orders directly from their ERP systems. This, in turn, automatically generates a production order within Endress Hauser’s ERP system and forwards it to the production facility. 
    • Aligned priority use case: Deliver Connected Experiences - Accelerate integration and increase productivity
  • Analyze: ARAG adopted SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud to create a unified and efficient analytics solution, enabling easy data accessibility. The combination of these technologies enabled ARAG to make all their data more accessible across different countries and teams, which paved the way for faster gaining of meaningful insights.
    • Aligned priority use case 1: Integrate hyperscaler data with SAP Datasphere - Deliver insights using live data from SAP and hyperscalers
    • Aligned priority use case 2: Accelerate Financial Planning and Analysis - Enhance SAP S/4HANA with planning and analysis
  • Automate processes: Orbico's IT team implemented SAP Build Process Automation to automate specific processes and tasks, thereby reducing project times and costs. The team launched over 40 process automation projects within a year, including a bot that automated invoice processing from PDFs into SAP S/4HANA, recognizing invoices from 20 vendors.
    • Aligned priority use case: Increase efficiency and accuracy while reducing costs - Automate processes to empower finance professionals
  • Embed intelligence: To Mercedes-AMG PETRONAS Formula One Team, efficiency is key to success. Formula One has cost caps that limit each team’s spending on cars per season with severe penalties for overshoots, making financial management one of their biggest challenges. Mercedes-AMG PETRONAS Team will leverage SAP S/4HANA Finance and SAP Business AI to optimize resource management and cost forecasting.
    • Aligned priority use case: Enhance customer support using the power of generative AI - Improve operations and decision-making with the power of generative AI

 

How to get started

SAP Discovery Center is an incredible asset to any business. If you’re in search of ways to start deriving value with SAP Business Technology Platform for your business, this is the place to start. It empowers users to easily uncover, find, and embark on SAP Business Technology Platform use cases.

Why SAP Discovery Center?

  1. SAP Discovery Center offers a catalogue of hundreds of detailed and comprehensive missions. These missions cover numerous aspects of business to inspire you on your SAP BTP journey. For example, when you’re ready to embark on the Autonomous Finance journey, these Discovery Center Missions will help you to take each step and make it best suited to your business needs.
  2. It provides guidance on how to use the wide range of services offered by SAP. This step-by-step guidance means you can use SAP’s powerful suite of tools to the best of your abilities and utilize the Free Tier and/or Free Trial.
  3. It encourages learning and exploration. With SAP Discovery Center, you not only understand how a host of innovative technologies could potentially benefit your business but also how to implement them efficiently for the best results with the provided reference architecture.
  4. The collaborative feature of SAP Discovery Center is very important. Knowledge and experiences are shared, thus enhancing productivity and enabling synergy. You can see which existing SAP customers have already implemented the use case of your interest and connect with SAP experts if you have any questions on the Mission.

Using SAP Discovery Center means you’re leveraging not just a tool but a collaborative platform that guides innovation, encourages learning, and boosts efficiency. Make sure you're tuned in for the XP180 | Get started with SAP BTP: Discover and accelerate with SAP Discovery Center session by Nis Boy Naeve and Ingo Deck from SAP Discovery Center team to learn more. 

 

What else can I do with SAP Business Technology Platform?

If you are interested in understanding further what SAP Business Technology Platform can do for your business, check out our comprehensive use case one-pager. The document is designed to provide a clear outline of how SAP BTP can be utilized to enhance your business operations, per your department. Grasping the use cases will allow your business to leverage SAP BTP effectively to drive business performance and inform your strategy, discover new potential, and maximize your technology investment with our SAP BTP use case one-pager. Don’t forget to also check out SAP Business Technology Platform use cases on the website.

You can read further on the SAP Business Technology Platform Priority Use Case One-Pager on this blog article.

 

Get ready for SAP TechEd 2024

I hope this got you excited about SAP TechEd 2024. Don't miss this opportunity to be part of a transformative event, and make sure to register for the keynote session with Muhammad Alam. Register now to secure your spot, and add the following recommended sessions to your agenda.

  • DT100 | Unlocking the intelligent enterprise: Cloud ERP innovations for the future
  • DA180 | Explore SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud end to end
  • IN103 | Modernize and elevate to the cloud with SAP Integration Suite
  • XP206 | Achieve enterprise automation with SAP BTP solutions and SAP Signavio
  • AI194 | SAP Business AI and Joule: What’s in it for innovators? 
  • XP180 | Get started with SAP BTP: Discover and accelerate with SAP Discovery Center

 

We look forward to connecting with you!
